description: Patch release: adds Oh My Pi platform support and fixes Pi/OMP context injection, ZCode layout, channel session reliability, and task CLI hygiene.
changelog:
**Enhancements:**
- feat(omp): add Oh My Pi platform support. `trellis init --omp` writes `.omp/agents/`, `.omp/commands/`, `.omp/skills/`, and `.omp/extensions/trellis/`, and registers OMP in `cli_adapter.py` / `task_store.py` so OMP projects receive Trellis workflow and sub-agent context.

**Bug Fixes:**
- fix(pi): keep Pi `systemPrompt` byte-stable across turns by memoizing startup/task context and delivering mutable workflow/session/task updates through persistent hidden messages, preserving provider prefix-cache eligibility.
- fix(omp): add rich session-start, task, and sub-agent context injection in the OMP extension, use `pi/task` model hints for implement/research agents, include YAML frontmatter in generated command files, and avoid stale session identity fallback.
- fix(zcode): write ZCode-managed skills to `.zcode/skills/`, complete `.zcode/agents/` with `trellis-research`, treat `.zcode` as sub-agent-capable for JSONL seed generation, and keep sub-agents under `.zcode/agents/` instead of legacy `.zcode/cli/agents/`.
- fix(channel): resolve Windows npm `.cmd` / node-script shims before spawning Codex channel workers and serialize stdout event appends so concurrent channel output cannot corrupt event records.
- fix(cli): ignore stale `task.json.branch` when stamping session journals, falling back to the live checkout branch when the recorded task branch no longer exists.
- fix(task): guard explicit `task.py create --slug` values that already include an `MM-DD-` prefix, warn and normalize today's prefix, and reject other valid date prefixes before creating doubled task directory names.
- fix(task): warn on blank `task.py create --description`, normalize whitespace-only descriptions to `""`, make automatic session activation visible, and add `--no-start` for backlog creation without moving the session pointer.
- fix(codex): make `.codex/` seed `implement.jsonl` / `check.jsonl` only when `codex.dispatch_mode: sub-agent` is explicitly configured; Codex inline mode now skips JSONL seed files.
- fix(session): replace placeholder journal defaults with explicit fallback text and correct generated update hints to recommend `trellis update`.
- fix(copilot): preserve repo-authored `.github/copilot-instructions.md` content while managing only the Trellis guidance block during init/update/uninstall.
- fix(hooks): keep shared hook failure handling fail-open without catching `BaseException`.

**Migration:** ZCode users who initialized with `--zcode` on 0.6.3-0.6.5 should run `trellis update --migrate` once to relocate `.zcode/cli/agents/` to `.zcode/agents/` if the legacy directory exists. New installs write the corrected `.zcode/skills/` and `.zcode/agents/` layout directly.

migrations.description: ZCode: move installed sub-agents from the legacy `.zcode/cli/agents/` directory to the new `.zcode/agents/` discovery path.
migrations.reason: ZCode dropped the intermediate `cli/` segment from its sub-agent discovery path. Trellis now writes to `.zcode/agents/`; this relocation keeps already-initialized projects consistent without leaving a stale directory behind.

notes:
No `--migrate` required for new installs.

Run `trellis update` after upgrading to refresh generated scripts, hooks, platform templates, and task workflow guidance.

ZCode users on 0.6.3-0.6.5 should run `trellis update --migrate` once if `.zcode/cli/agents/` exists; this moves legacy sub-agents to `.zcode/agents/`. `trellis update` will also add `.zcode/skills/` and `trellis-research` through normal template updates.

Oh My Pi users can run `trellis init --omp` in projects that do not yet have `.omp/`; existing OMP dogfood projects should run `trellis update`.

Pi users should run `trellis update` to receive the cache-stable extension runtime. Codex inline users should run `trellis update` so new tasks stop receiving seed-only JSONL files.

Install: `npm install -g @mindfoldhq/trellis`
